summary: Corrected Round 9 landed the NoETL GUI gateway-session auth pattern in noetl/travel and updated the public tutorial. Cloudflare Access remains cancelled. Travel now exchanges the Auth0 ID token with gateway.mestumre.dev/api/auth/login for a gateway session_token, stores session_token/user_info in localStorage like repos/gui, uses Authorization: Bearer <session_token> for gateway execution, and disables production guest mode by default. Code and docs PRs are merged and Travel Pages deploy is green. Final GREEN is pending Kadyapam's browser smoke.

request_shape.auth0_token: Auth0 ID token; Travel uses getIdTokenClaims().__raw, not getAccessTokenSilently()

gui_pattern_summary.401_handling: clear local session state and return user to sign-in flow

kadyapam_smoke_checklist: Open https://travel.mestumre.dev/ in an incognito window.
Expect a sign-in pane, not the chat shell and not a Guest mode path.
Click Sign in and complete Auth0 as the allowed user.
Expect a brief 'Linking to gateway...' state.
Expect the chat shell to load with the signed-in user identity visible.
Start a trip, for example 'Find flights to Paris'.
In browser devtools, verify gateway requests carry Authorization: Bearer <session_token>.
Confirm widgets render after the gateway-mediated playbook execution.
cleanup: No Cloudflare Access app or policy was created. Superseded Cloudflare Access artifacts remain for audit only. Unrelated untracked Amadeus handoff files were left untouched.
verdict: AMBER pending Kadyapam browser smoke. Code, docs, merge, Auth0 default hotfix, and Cloudflare Pages deploy are complete.
